key_insight: Quantum state tomography can be made scalable and physically valid by estimating a structured factor F and reconstructing the density matrix as FF†, unifying low-rank, tensor-network, and neural representations in one framework.
engineering_relevance: The framework offers a practical route to reduce sample and parameter complexity in quantum reconstruction, with algorithms that can be deployed across different measurement and model settings.
method: Parameterize the density matrix as FF† to enforce positivity and unit trace constraints by construction.
Choose F from structured model classes such as Cholesky, low-rank matrices, MPOs, or neural density operators.
Use projected gradient descent or a step-size-free power method for optimization from measurement data.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a unified statistical analysis of least-squares sample complexity and proposes optimization methods for reconstruction, including a special-case recovery of Cover's method.
watch_item: Performance and convergence will depend on how well the chosen factor structure matches the true quantum state, so model mismatch and optimization nonconvexity remain practical concerns.
paper_title: Structured Factorization Approaches for Quantum State Tomography
